_meta.experiment: #96 TQ3P-vs-TQ2P decode thread-scaling — reconcile the #63 pod "TQ3P ~90x slower" against the EPYC llama-bench 1.35x
_meta.model: Qwen3-1.7B GGUF, sasori TQ2P/TQ3P kernel, llama.cpp b9509 + patch
_meta.host: RunPod AMD Threadripper PRO 5955WX, 16 physical / 32 logical cores; llama-bench -n 24 -r 3; GPU idle (clean)
verdict: At threads <= physical cores (16), TQ3P is healthy: ~1.5x TQ2P TIME (0.67x throughput), the expected 3-plane-vs-2-plane ratio, NOT 90x.
Both kernels collapse under thread OVERSUBSCRIPTION; TQ3P collapses EARLIER (t=24 = 1.5x physical) than TQ2P (t=32 = 2x physical) because TQ3P is more memory-bandwidth-bound.

The #63 pod "TQ3P 1.5 tok/s vs TQ2P 138 tok/s (~90x)" was a 64-core COMMUNITY pod running llama-server with oversubscribed/contended threads, hitting exactly the asymmetric-collapse regime where TQ3P craters and TQ2P still runs.

CONCLUSION: the ~90x is a thread-oversubscription/contention ARTIFACT, not a TQ3P kernel defect. At correct thread settings (<=physical), TQ3P decode ~= 1.5x TQ2P time. llama-server picks physical cores by default (verified locally: 6 of 12).
_meta.note_collapse: null = COLLAPSED (>100s for a single 24-token point = <0.24 tok/s). tq2p@32 measured separately (timeout 120s).
